scenario.title: Onboarding — 3 Questions, Done
scenario.description: New contractor texts in. Entire onboarding is name+trade, business name, hourly rate. Then they're in.
scenario.onboarding_summary: 3 questions. Name+trade, business name, rate. Done.

steps.response: Onboarding complete. 3 exchanges.

onboarding_log.text:
Hey, I'm FIELDHAND — your AI business assistant. Handles your invoices, quotes, permits, and client follow-ups.

What's your name and trade? (e.g. 'Jake Morales, electrician')

Got it, Jake. What's your business name?
(Or just your name if you're solo)
What's your hourly labor rate? (just the number, e.g. 110)

You're in, Jake! Here's what you can text me:

• "New job, Smith, 412 Maple St, panel upgrade"
• "Quote Smith — 200A panel $210, 8 hours labor, 25% markup"
• "Smith job is done. Send the invoice."

I'll ask for anything else as we go. What's your first job?

input.subject: Quote for 200A Panel Upgrade - 412 Maple St
input.body:
Hi Dave,

Thanks for reaching out about the 200 amp panel upgrade at 412 Maple St. Here's your quote:

MATERIALS:
• 200A Square D panel - $262.50
• 15 breakers @ $11.56 each - $173.40
• 6 AFCIs @ $52.50 each - $315.00

LABOR:
• 8 hours @ $110/hr - $880.00

TOTAL: $1,630.90

This quote is good for 30 days. Let me know if you have any questions or if you'd like to move forward.

Best,
Jake Morales
Morales Electric LLC
input.context: Quote for 200A panel upgrade at Dave Smith's property

scenario.title: Business Analysis — Why Losing Margin? (Tier 3)
scenario.description: Complex investigation query. Agent uses query_business tool with extended thinking.
scenario.message: I feel like I'm losing money on some jobs. Can you dig into my profitability and tell me what's going on?

input.question: Analyze profitability across all jobs. Which jobs are losing money or have thin margins? Break down actual costs vs quoted amounts, and identify any patterns in expenses or budget overruns.

tool_calls.result:
PROFITABILITY ANALYSIS (2 jobs with quotes):
  +89% | $1,953 quoted / $210 cost | 200A panel upgrade - Smith (Dave Smith)
  +69% | $1,200 quoted / $368 cost | Kitchen remodel circuits - Gar (Maria Garcia)
Average margin: 79.3%
EXPENSE BREAKDOWN ($578.00 total):
  materials: $578.00 (100%)

steps.response:
Actually, your margins look **great** on paper — 89% on Smith, 69% on Garcia. But here's the catch:

Those numbers only count **materials** you've logged ($578 total). They don't include your **labor hours**.

If you spent, say, 10 hours on Garcia (10 × $110 = $1,100), your real cost is $1,468 — leaving you only $32 profit instead of $832.

**Bottom line:** You're probably not losing money, but you're not making what you think either because labor time isn't tracked.

Want me to start tracking hours per job so you can see true profitability?

input.query: 200 amp panel upgrade permit requirements Manchester NH

    Overview of Electrical Panel Replacement Permits and Regulations Electrical panel replacement permits are required in most jurisdictions across the United States and are essential for safety compliance, legal protection, and insurance coverage. Whether you're upgrading from a 100-amp to 200-amp electrical service or replacing an outdated panel, obtaining the necessary permits ensures your ...

A thorough service upgrade may also involve upgrading the meter socket, wiring, and grounding system. Regardless of the extent of your ...

steps.response:
Here's what I found for **Manchester, NH**:

**Building Department Contact:**
- Planning & Community Development, Building Regulations Division
- One City Hall Plaza, Manchester, NH 03101
- Phone: (603) 624-6475
- Email: pcd@manchesternh.gov

**Permit Process:**
- Electrical permits are handled through the Building Department
- Applications can be submitted online or by mail/in-person

**What I couldn't find:**
- Specific permit fees for electrical work
- Inspection requirements or timeline
- Whether a licensed electrician affidavit is needed

**Recommendation:** Call (603) 624-6475 and ask for:
- Permit fee for 200A service upgrade
- Required docs (your license, scope of work, etc.)
- Inspection schedule

Want me to generate a permit prep sheet for the Smith job once you get the requirements?

scenario.title: Overdue Check
scenario.description: Invoices aged. Agent spots them.
scenario.message: Any invoices I should be chasing?

steps.response:
**Yes — Smith.**

There's an invoice for **$1,952.94** (same amount as the one we just sent) showing as sent on **March 14** — **20 days overdue**.

That's confusing, because you just told me the Smith job is done and we sent an invoice today.

**What happened:**
- Was there an earlier invoice (deposit or progress billing)?
- Or is this stale data in the system?

Either way, if Dave owes you $1,952.94 from March 14, that's 20 days past Net 15. You should call him.

Want me to draft a payment reminder?

steps.response:
**Active jobs:**

1. **Kitchen remodel circuits - Garcia**
   - Client: Maria Garcia
   - Quoted: $1,200
   - Spent so far: $368 (31% of budget)
   - Address: 88 Pine Ave

2. **200A panel upgrade - Smith**
   - Client: Dave Smith
   - Quoted: $1,952.94
   - Spent so far: $210 (11% of budget)
   - Address: 412 Maple St

Both are tracking under budget. Garcia is about a third through, Smith just getting started on materials.

Need to update status on either of these, or want details on one?
